author: Dr. Anita Joshi
content:
PRIMARY CARE — WORK PHYSICAL

SUBJECTIVE: 42-year-old construction foreman for a routine employment physical. Feels well. No chest pain, dyspnea, leg swelling, or bleeding problems. No prior blood clots. Former smoker (quit 2014). Social alcohol. Very active through work.

OBJECTIVE: BP 126/80, HR 72, BMI ~29. Exam unremarkable. Labs (LAB_HX_001): normal CBC, renal and hepatic function, A1c 5.4, LDL borderline.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Healthy adult male — cleared for work.
#Overweight — counseled on diet/activity.
#Health maintenance — influenza vaccine; routine screening age-appropriate.
No chronic medications. No anticoagulation. No known liver disease.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Anita Joshi, MD. 9/18/2026.
